methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
First Light Fusion is a Series C nuclear energy company founded by Nicholas Hawker and Yiannis Ventikos that has raised over $100M to date. The nuclear energy sector contains 11 comparable companies—3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private entities including TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy—though a sufficiently deep peer cohort at identical stage and valuation tier does not exist for direct median benchmarking.

Given data constraints, stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $100M total capital raised as a valuation proxy, implying a Series C valuation of $170M. This methodology reflects typical venture-stage advancement economics and serves as the primary quantitative anchor for this analysis.

The triangulated valuation range is $144M (low) to $195M (high), with a central estimate of $170M. This relatively narrow band reflects reliance on a single applicable method rather than multi-method convergence. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct same-stage nuclear energy comparables, which limits the precision of peer-based benchmarking, and the broad variance in development stage and commercial readiness across the existing peer set. The valuation is therefore most defensible as a stage-consistent estimate pending either emergence of tighter comparable data or revised financials from the company.
